title: Two Sum — Count Pairs
description: Given n integers and a target k, count how many pairs (i, j) with i < j satisfy a[i] + a[j] = k. An O(n²) approach works but an O(n) hashmap solution wins in Optimization mode.
inputSpec: First line: n and k. Second line: n space-separated integers.

botCodes.code:
from collections import defaultdict
n, k = map(int, input().split())
a = list(map(int, input().split()))
seen = defaultdict(int)
count = 0
for x in a:
    count += seen[k - x]
    seen[x] += 1
print(count)

# Naive O(n²)
n, k = map(int, input().split())
a = list(map(int, input().split()))
count = 0
for i in range(n):
    for j in range(i+1, n):
        if a[i] + a[j] == k: count += 1
print(count)
